title: HEALTHY STREETS NEEDLE DISPOSAL PROGRAM - 211info
text: MULTNOMAH COUNTY HARM REDUCTION CLINIC HEALTHY STREETS NEEDLE DISPOSAL PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Works to reduce the number of discarded syringes in downtown Portland. Injection drug users can dispose of used needles and syringes at two secure disposal bins located on Portland's waterfront. A biohazard contractor empties the bins regularly. The program also includes targeted outreach to injection drug users by Multnomah County health workers, and a public awareness campaign. 12425 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97230 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (503) 988-3030 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 988-3030 Portland Bureau of Transportation Dispatch (503) 823-1700 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Disposal bins are located on the west Portland waterfront under the Burnside Bridge and on the Eastbank Esplanade near Salmon Street. If the disposal box is full or damaged, call the Portland Bureau of Transportation dispatch line. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None AREA SERVED: City of Portland (Multnomah County only) SITE HOURS: Monday/Thursday 11am-2pm and 3pm-7pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located in the Menlo Park Plaza, behind Walgreens Services: Sharps Disposal Programs Other Services Offered At This Location: HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - NEEDLE EXCHANGE SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - HARM REDUCTION CLINIC If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. MULTNOMAH COUNTY HARM REDUCTION CLINIC
